sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 4.77 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.4)

statement.latex: In 1972, A.\,Rudvalis discovered a new simple group$R$of order$2^{14} 3^3 5^3 7 13 29$. He has shown that$R$possesses an involution$i$such that$C_R(i)=V F$, where$V$is a 4-group (an elementary abelian group of order$4$) and$F Sz(8)$. Show that$R$is the only finite simple group$G$that possesses an involution$i$such that$C_G(i)=V F$, where$V$is a 4-group and$F Sz(8)$. Let$G$be a non-abelian finite s...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
